prompt: I have a Python training script at ~/ml/train_cifar.py that takes about 3 hours. I want to run it on a Colab GPU in the background so it survives if my laptop goes to sleep. What's the full setup? The script needs torch, torchvision, and wandb. It saves checkpoints to ./checkpoints/ every 10 epochs.

assertions.text: Tells user to create a GPU session with colab new --gpu
Tells user to upload script files with colab upload
Uses start_new_session or subprocess.Popen pattern for background execution
Mentions PYTHONUNBUFFERED or python -u for log output
Mentions installing torch, torchvision, wandb dependencies
Mentions using relative paths with colab exec -f
Mentions colab download to retrieve checkpoints
Shows how to check on running training (pgrep, log tail, or check_progress script)
